label: Contractors

seo.title: Contractor Funding for Materials, Payroll, and Growth
seo.description: Fast capital options for contractors covering materials, payroll gaps, and job growth.

hero.headline: Contractor Funding for Materials, Payroll, and Growth
hero.subheadline: When the job starts before the money lands, you need funding that understands supplier invoices, payroll timing, and project momentum.

problem: Materials get expensive before the first draw clears.
Payroll cannot wait on client payment cycles.
Generic lenders do not understand project-based cashflow.
solution: Match contractors with funding lanes built around deposits, invoices, and repeat project activity instead of slow bank paperwork.

proof.title: Project-based cashflow logic
proof.description: The page speaks to the way contractors actually collect money and manage jobs.

proof.title: Clear funding lanes
proof.description: Fast options, growth options, and prep paths are separated so the buyer can self-select.

proof.title: Lower friction CTA
proof.description: The page points to a short next step instead of a vague generic application.

howItWorks: Start with your job size, bank activity, and timeline.
Compare the fastest available funding lane against the lowest-friction lane.
Book the next step if the numbers and timing make sense.

useCases.title: Materials purchase
useCases.description: Use the page when you need to front-load supplies for a larger project.

useCases.title: Payroll bridge
useCases.description: Use it when crews need to be paid before the client releases funds.

useCases.title: Growth campaign
useCases.description: Use it for ads, outbound, or SEO when you want more contractor leads.

objections.a: Not always. Many contractor-friendly options care more about deposits and project activity.

objections.q: What if my jobs are seasonal?
objections.a: That is common. The point is to match the funding lane to the rhythm of the business.

objections.q: Can this help with emergency jobs?
objections.a: Yes. The page is built to surface faster options when timing matters more than rate shopping.

finalCta.label: Book a Contractor Review
finalCta.description: Use this page to turn project cashflow into a faster capital conversation.
